tasks.description: Full-stack implementation of `sotp verify doc-hidden` gate — infrastructure syn framework, doc_hidden module, adapter, usecase port extension, CLI dispatch, and Makefile CI wiring — as a single feature batch. (1) `libs/infrastructure/src/verify/syn_helpers.rs`: add `pub(crate) fn scan_rs_files(root: &Path, on_file: impl FnMut(&Path, &syn::File) -> Vec<VerifyFinding>) -> VerifyOutcome` (or equivalent callback-based API) that recursively discovers `.rs` files, parses each with `syn::parse_file`, excludes items inside `#[cfg(test)]` blocks and `#[test]` functions at the syn AST level using the existing `has_cfg_test_attr`, and collects all returned findings into a `VerifyOutcome`. (2) Create `libs/infrastructure/src/verify/doc_hidden.rs`: `pub fn verify(root: &Path) -> VerifyOutcome` that reads `architecture-rules.json` `layers[]` to enumerate crate source dirs at runtime (CN-02), calls the scan framework from (1) with a callback that detects `pub + #[doc(hidden)]` on each item at the syn AST level (CN-04), and on violation emits a `VerifyFinding::error` containing file path, item name, and prohibition reason ("pub + #[doc(hidden)] is forbidden: rustdoc excludes this item from its paths output, causing TDDD chain③ to fire DanglingId and block the track-active-gate commit gate") (CN-05, AC-01). Non-pub `#[doc(hidden)]` items must not be reported (OS-04). `#[cfg(test)]` / `#[test]` scoped items must not be reported (IN-06, AC-04). Add unit tests: (a) a tmpdir with no violations returns ok (AC-02); (b) a file containing `#[doc(hidden)] pub fn foo()` returns an error finding with file path and item name (AC-01, CN-05); (c) a `#[cfg(test)] mod tests { #[doc(hidden)] pub fn bar() {} }` block is not reported (AC-04, IN-06); (d) a `#[test] #[doc(hidden)] pub fn baz() {}` item is not reported (AC-04, IN-06); (e) a `#[doc(hidden)] fn private()` (no pub) is not reported (OS-04). (3) `libs/infrastructure/src/verify/mod.rs`: add `pub mod doc_hidden;`. (4) `libs/infrastructure/src/verify_adapter.rs`: add `verify_doc_hidden(&self, project_root: &Path) -> Result<VerifyOutcome, VerifyPortError>` to `FsVerifyAdapter`, implementing the `VerifyPort` contract by calling `render_outcome("verify doc-hidden", &crate::verify::doc_hidden::verify(project_root))` after the standard `reject_symlinked_trusted_root` guard. (5) `libs/usecase/src/verify.rs`: add `verify_doc_hidden(&self, project_root: &Path) -> Result<VerifyOutcome, VerifyPortError>` to the `VerifyPort` trait; add `verify_doc_hidden(&self, project_root: PathBuf) -> Result<VerifyOutcome, VerifyPortError>` to the `VerifyService` trait; add the delegation method to `VerifyInteractor`. (6) `apps/cli-driver/src/verify.rs`: add `DocHidden { project_root: PathBuf }` variant to `VerifyInput`; add the dispatch arm `VerifyInput::DocHidden { project_root } => map_result(self.service.verify_doc_hidden(project_root))` in `VerifyDriver::handle`. (7) `apps/cli/src/commands/verify.rs`: add `DocHidden(VerifyArgs)` variant to `VerifyCommand` with doc comment "Check all architecture-rules.json layers[] crates for pub + #[doc(hidden)] violations."; add arm to `items_dir()` matching the pattern for project-root-based commands; add dispatch arm in `dispatch_to_outcome`. (8) `Makefile.toml`: add `[tasks.verify-doc-hidden-local]` (private, script: `cargo run --quiet -p cli -- verify doc-hidden`) and compose wrapper `[tasks.verify-doc-hidden]`; insert `"verify-doc-hidden-local"` into the `dependencies` arrays of both `[tasks.ci-local]` and `[tasks.ci-container]`. Note: no changes to domain layer, cli_composition layer, or existing verify gate behavior; only shared syn_helpers.rs and the new doc_hidden module change under libs/infrastructure/src/verify (CN-01, AC-06, GO-03). This entire batch fits within per-scope diff ceilings (infrastructure ≈200 lines, usecase ≈30 lines, cli_driver ≈15 lines, cli ≈25 lines, harness-policy ≈15 lines — all under the 500-line ceiling).

summary: Single feature-batch task: implement the `sotp verify doc-hidden` gate end-to-end across infrastructure (syn framework + doc_hidden module + adapter), usecase (port + interactor), cli_driver (VerifyInput), cli (VerifyCommand), and Makefile.toml (CI wiring). All per-scope diffs are under the 500-line ceiling so no batch split is needed.

Track-wide guardrail: do not introduce new `pub + #[doc(hidden)]` in any implementation file. This is exactly the pattern the new gate forbids; the implementation must comply with the rule it enforces.

sections.title: doc-hidden gate: full-stack implementation (infrastructure + usecase + CLI + Makefile)
description: Extend syn_helpers.rs with a reusable callback-based file-scan framework (IN-01, GO-01).
Create verify/doc_hidden.rs implementing pub fn verify(root) using the framework and architecture-rules.json layers[] (IN-02, IN-05, GO-02).
Register the module in verify/mod.rs and implement verify_doc_hidden on FsVerifyAdapter (IN-02, IN-03).
Extend VerifyPort + VerifyService traits and VerifyInteractor delegation in libs/usecase (IN-03).
Add DocHidden variant + dispatch to VerifyInput (cli_driver) and VerifyCommand (cli) (IN-03).
Wire verify-doc-hidden-local into ci-local and ci-container in Makefile.toml (IN-04, AC-05).

Unit tests for the doc_hidden module cover local detection behavior and AST-based exclusions; CI wiring, layer enumeration, and existing-gate constraints are covered by the implementation wiring and verification commands.
